calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: SANCTIONS
Off Calendar Sep-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: SANCTIONS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court has received no response from judgment debtor Vincent Vandell to its order to show cause why Vandell should not be sanctioned for failure to comply with the court's May 5, 2025 discovery order. The court orders Vandell to pay $1000 to the court within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. The court will prepare the order.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Stephen M. Williams In Support Of Second Motion To Compel Responses To Special And Form Interrogatories, Set One, To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set One And For Compliance With The Courts Prior Order And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF STEPHEN WILLIAMS' Motion To Compel Responses To Special And Form Interrogatories, Set One, To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set One And For Compliance With The Courts Prior Order And For Monetary Sanctions.

The unopposed motion to compel responses to discovery is granted and the court sets the matter on an order to show cause for September 25, 2025.

The judgment debtor has not served responses and has not abided by the court's prior order to make objection-free responses. The judgment debtor is ordered to make objection-free responses within 10 days of entry of this order and to pay $60 to the judgment creditor within 30 days of entry of this order.

The judgment creditor asks for a much larger sanction in view of the judgment debtor's failure to abide by a prior order. The court issues an order to show cause to Vincent Vandell and his counsel why they should not be sanctioned $1000, payable to the court, for failure to comply with the court's May 5, 2025 discovery order. The OSC shall be set for hearing on September 25, 2025 at 9 am in Department 301. A response to the OSC may be filed no later than five court days before the hearing date. The judgment creditor is ordered to serve a copy of this order on the judgment debtor and to file his proof of service with the court.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar May-16-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses
Off Calendar May-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF STEPHEN WILLIAMS' Motion To Compel Discovery Responses

The motion to compel discovery responses and for sanctions is granted in part and denied in part.

The court exercises its discretion to consider defendant Vincent Vandell's late-filed opposition. If plaintiff Stephen Williams contests this tentative, the court will continue the matter for hearing to enable Williams to submit a reply.

Based on the moving papers and opposition, the court's indicated order is to grant the motion and compel objection-free responses to the form interrogatories, special interrogatories, and requests for production, within 20 days of entry of this order. Vandell contends that post-judgment discovery is premature because he may yet appeal, but he offers no authority that the fact of a pending appeal window stays discovery. Indeed, even an appeal does not automatically stay enforcement of a money judgment. The court does not find substantial justification for the opposition to the discovery motion. The court's indicated order is to grant sanctions of $60 payable by Vandell to Williams within 20 days of entry of this order, representing Williams's filing fees incurred in bringing this motion. The court does not award attorneys' fees because Williams is self-represented and may not recover his ordinary fees as discovery sanctions. (See Argaman v. Ratan (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 1173, 1179.)

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

**After hearing, the Court prepares and signs an order **
